_note: ROUND 2 batch (undated): for round-1-added parents whose OWN label lacked a parseable year (mostly medieval/early-modern European nobility with no ancestry shown), this adds THEIR documented parents/grandparents plus full siblings, one generation further back (occasionally two, where the further generation is directly and cleanly documented in the same source). New nodes use the R2L_ prefix; unknown parents are '—' placeholders suffixed _MOTHER. Filled 20 of 30; skipped 10 as genuinely undocumented (see below). DEDUP: Egidia de Burgh's brother Richard Óg de Burgh, 2nd Earl of Ulster, already exists as SPJ_RICHARDDEBURGH (round-1 father of Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots) — referenced by id, not duplicated, linking two round-1 additions through their shared grandparents Walter de Burgh & Aveline FitzJohn. Adele of France's father is the EXISTING ROBERTIFR (Robert I of France), referenced by id (not a new node); this is a SEPARATE, unknown-mother union from the tree's existing U_robertifr_beatv (Robert I x Beatrice of Vermandois -> Hugh the Great), since Adele's maternity is not confirmed as Beatrice's. FLAGGED disputed/traditional identifications (source given in each note): Judith of Bavaria/Sülichgau's mother's identity (modern scholarship vs. traditional Judith of Friuli); Cassander (Antigone's father) is NOT the famous Diadoch king of the same name (he was Antipater's brother, not son); Margaret Graham's father (John Graham of Abercorn vs. Scots Peerage's David Graham of Montrose); Edith Stourton's mother (Alice, 'presumably'); Emilia Pannocchieschi vs. Wikipedia's 'Camilla Pannocchieschi' (same generational slot, different first name across sources, not resolved); Giovanni de' Bardi vs. Wikipedia's naming of his brother Alessandro as Contessina de' Bardi's actual father (round-1 data not altered, discrepancy flagged only); Theophylact Dalassenos as father of Adrianos Dalassenos is Wikipedia's own hedge ('most likely', not certain); Rollo's mother (Hildr/Ragnhild Hrólfsdóttir) is saga-only (Heimskringla/Orkneyinga Saga), matching the existing ⚠ flag already on SPE_ROGNVALD; Matilda of Château-du-Loir's grandparents (Robert 'Brochard' & Élisabeth) are sourced to genealogical compilations (FamilySearch/Geneanet) since Wikipedia's own article on Gervais II does not document his parents. SKIPPED (undocumented parentage / unnamed placeholders, no fabrication): SPE_EADBURH (Asser names no specific father; modern historians propose conflicting, unconsensused candidates — Abels' descent from Cenwulf vs. Mommaerts-Browne's descent from Wigmund); SPE_ALEXIOSCHARON ('very little is known of his life' per Bryennios, no parents recorded); SPL_LOTWIFE (Genesis 19:26 names no parents); SPH_BILLUNG_FRANK (Vita Mathildis traditional figure, no further ancestry given); SPE_AETHELREDMUCEL (a father 'Mucil' is only speculative — 'hardly anything is known for certain'); SPE_ANDRONIKOSDOUKAS_ELDER (Constantine X's father's own parents are not named in any primary source); SPE_MALCI_MOTHER (unnamed '—' placeholder, no identity to research); SPE_JOHNDOUKAS_MOTHER (unnamed '—' placeholder); SPG_MAGASMAC (Wikipedia: 'an obscure local', no parents documented); SPH_AEDA (Vita Mathildis traditional figure, no further ancestry given). Web-verified July 2026 against Wikipedia (English and French) and, where flagged, genealogical compilations (Geneanet, FamilySearch, WikiTree, soc.genealogy.medieval).

a.label:
Count Eberhard of Sülichgau
d.after 889 · count in the Sülichgau · Wikipedia

b.label:
Gisela of Verona
fl.880s · wife of Count Eberhard of Sülichgau · Wikipedia

families.note: Judith, wife of Arnulf 'the Bad', Duke of Bavaria: modern scholarship (Wikipedia 'Judith, Duchess of Bavaria') identifies her as daughter of Count Eberhard of Sülichgau & Gisela of Verona, displacing the traditional identification as Judith of Friuli (chronologically impossible — Judith of Friuli died c.881, decades before children are attested). ⚠ disputed identification, flagged in label. No documented siblings for this Judith found.

a.label:
Cassander
· obscure Macedonian nobleman, brother of Antipater · not the Diadoch king of the same name · Wikipedia

families.note: Antigone (niece of Antipater, wife of Magas, mother of Berenice I) was daughter of Cassander, a minor Macedonian noble who was Antipater's BROTHER — Wikipedia ('Berenice I') does not confirm or deny whether this is the same person as the far more famous King Cassander (Antipater's SON, already in the tree as MAC_CASSANDER); since the relationships differ (brother vs. son) they are treated as distinct, and the ambiguity is flagged in the label rather than assumed. Mother unrecorded.

families.note: Egidia de Burgh's parents are Walter de Burgh, 1st Earl of Ulster, & Aveline FitzJohn (Wikipedia 'Walter de Burgh, 1st Earl of Ulster'). DEDUP: her brother Richard Óg de Burgh, 2nd Earl of Ulster, is the EXISTING SPJ_RICHARDDEBURGH (round-1 father of Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots) — referenced by id, not duplicated, so this links two round-1 additions through their shared grandparents. Full brothers Theobald, William, Thomas also documented by Wikipedia.

families.note: Giovanni de' Bardi (1354-1442), Count of Vernio, was son of Sozzo de' Bardi, Signore di Vernio, & Beatrice Mozzi, with full siblings Alessandro, Simone, and Francesca (Geneanet 'Sozzo de' Bardi' family tree). ⚠ FLAG: current Wikipedia ('Contessina de' Bardi') names his brother Alessandro — not Giovanni — as the father of Contessina de' Bardi (by Camilla Pannocchieschi); this discrepancy with the tree's existing round-1 assignment (Giovanni x Emilia Pannocchieschi -> Contessina) is noted but NOT altered here, as correcting round-1 attributions is out of scope for this batch.

a.label:
Raniero di Guido Pannocchieschi
· Count of Elci · Wikipedia

families.note: Per Wikipedia ('Contessina de' Bardi'), the Pannocchieschi wife of the Bardi Count of Vernio (mother of Contessina) was daughter of Raniero di Guido Pannocchieschi, Count of Elci. ⚠ FLAG: Wikipedia names her Camilla Pannocchieschi, not Emilia as already in this tree (round-1) — treated here as the same person/generational slot, first-name discrepancy flagged rather than resolved. Mother unrecorded.

Full siblings Boson II (succeeding viscount) and Magne, per French Wikipedia 'Raymond Ier de Turenne'.

families.note: Edith Stourton's father was Sir John Stourton (d.1438); her mother is only 'presumed' (FamilySearch, not certain) to be his 2nd wife Alice (d.1407) — flagged. Full sibling (by the same father, likely Alice) Margaret Stourton, Abbess of Shaftesbury (Wikipedia 'John Stourton (died 1438)'); an elder HALF-brother William Stourton (by the father's FIRST wife) is excluded as not a full sibling.

a.label:
Sir John de Graham
· of Abercorn · Wikipedia (⚠ Scots Peerage instead names Sir David Graham of Montrose)

families.note: Margaret Graham's father is given as Sir John de Graham of Abercorn per Wikipedia 'Hugh, Earl of Ross'; J. Balfour Paul's Scots Peerage instead names her as a daughter of Sir David Graham of Montrose — a documented scholarly disagreement, flagged rather than silently resolved. Mother unrecorded. No documented siblings.

families.note: Marged ferch Tomos was daughter of Tomos (Thomas) ap Llywelyn, Lord of Deheubarth (1302-1343); her full sister Elen ferch Tomos married Gruffudd Fychan ap Gruffudd ap Madog Crupl and was the mother of Owain Glyndŵr, per Wikipedia 'Tudur ap Goronwy' / 'Maredudd ap Tudur'. Mother unrecorded.

a.label:
Rothard

families.note: Welf I, Count of Altdorf (c.776-825, father of Empress Judith and of the tree's existing Emma of Altdorf sibling-pair via SPG_HEILWIG), was son of Rothard of the Argengau, per Wikipedia 'Welf (father of Judith)'. No documented siblings for Welf I. Mother unrecorded.

a.label:
Hardrad
fl.740s · grandfather of Welf I, Count of Altdorf · Wikipedia

families.note: Rothard of the Argengau (Welf I's father) was himself son of Hardrad, per Wikipedia 'Welf (father of Judith)' ('son of Rothard of the Argengau and grandson of Hardrad') — added here as the grandparent generation for SPG_WELF1ALTDORF.

a.label:

families.note: Heilwig (Hedwig) of Saxony, wife of Welf I (later Abbess of Chelles), was daughter of the Saxon count Isanbard (Isambart), per Wikipedia 'Welf (father of Judith)'. No documented siblings. Mother unrecorded.

a.label:

families.note: Teresa Lourenço's parents were Lourenço and Sancha Martins, both Lisbon merchants, per Wikipedia 'Teresa Lourenço' ('Her parents were Lourenço and Sancha Martins, who were merchants'). No documented siblings.

a.label:

· of Knightstone, Ottery St Mary, Devon · WikiTree/soc.genealogy.medieval

b.label:
Joan Beauchamp
· dau. & co-heiress of William Beauchamp of Wellington, Somerset · WikiTree/soc.genealogy.medieval

families.note: Joan Bittlesgate's parents were Thomas Bittlesgate, Esq., of Knightstone, Devon, & Joan Beauchamp, dau. and co-heiress of William Beauchamp of Wellington, Somerset — per the Complete Peerage correction thread (soc.genealogy.medieval mailing list) and WikiTree. No documented siblings.

Adele's father is the EXISTING ROBERTIFR (👑 Robert I of France), referenced by id, not duplicated. Her identification remains contested: Werner and Settipani argue the 'Adèle' who married Herbert II of Vermandois may have been Robert I's DAUGHTER (as shown here) rather than his WIFE, by an unrecorded mother — this is kept as a SEPARATE union from the tree's existing U_robertifr_beatv (Robert I x Beatrice of Vermandois -> Hugh the Great), since Adele's maternity is not established as Beatrice's. ⚠ disputed/traditional, per Wikipedia 'Robert I of France' (already flagged in the round-1 label).

No documented siblings for her; mother unrecorded.

a.label:
Robert 'Brochard'
· Lord of Château-du-Loir · Geneanet/FamilySearch

families.note: Grandparent generation for Matilda of Château-du-Loir: Gervais II's own parents are named on Geneanet/FamilySearch as Robert 'Brochard', Lord of Château-du-Loir, & Élisabeth. Wikipedia's own article on Gervais II does not document his parents, so this generation is sourced to genealogical compilations only — flagged as lower-certainty than the Wikipedia-sourced entries in this batch.
